title: Admin Layout Overhaul: Collapsible Drawer + Command Center + Split Focus
description:
Restructure admin page layouts with three key patterns:
1. Collapsible Drawer sidebar for ALL pages (replaces current fixed sidebar)
2. Command Center dashboard for admin home page (/admin)
3. Split Focus master-detail for employee and student list/edit views

This improves workflow efficiency by:
- Maximizing content width with collapsible nav
- Giving admins a bird's-eye overview dashboard
- Enabling rapid cycling through records without losing list context

title: Phase 1: CollapsibleSidebar component
description:
Create a new CollapsibleSidebar component to replace the current SidebarNav.

Current state:
- SidebarNav (app/ui/components/SidebarNav.tsx) renders a fixed 256px sidebar on desktop, Sheet on mobile
- Used by AdminShell and AppShell

New component behavior:
- Default state: collapsed icon-only rail (~48px wide)
- Hover OR click pin: expands to full width (~220px) with labels
- Smooth CSS transition (width + opacity on labels)
- Active route indicator (left border accent)
- Grouped sections with dividers (same groups API as current SidebarNav)
- Mobile: same Sheet behavior as today (no change)
- Pin/unpin toggle button at bottom of sidebar to lock expanded state
- Persist pinned state in localStorage

Files to create/modify:
- NEW: app/ui/components/CollapsibleSidebar.tsx
- MODIFY: app/ui/shells/AdminShell.tsx (swap SidebarNav for CollapsibleSidebar)
- MODIFY: app/ui/shells/AppShell.tsx (swap SidebarNav for CollapsibleSidebar)
- Keep SidebarNav around for backward compat (can deprecate later)

Props interface should match current SidebarNav groups API for drop-in replacement.

title: Phase 2: Admin Command Center dashboard
description:
Create an admin home/dashboard page at /admin (index route) with a Command Center layout.

Current state:
- No /admin index route exists. Hitting /admin shows nothing (Outlet with no match)
- Admins navigate directly to /admin/employees or /admin/students

New page: app/routes/admin/index.tsx

Loader fetches aggregate stats:
- Active/inactive employee counts
- Active/inactive student counts
- Expiring ID count (within 30 days)
- Expired ID count
- Missing photo counts (employees + students)
- Last sync timestamps (staff + student)

UI structure:
1. Top stat cards row (4 cards): Active Employees, Expiring IDs, Missing Photos, Last Sync
   - Each card is clickable, links to the relevant section
   - Color-coded: green/amber/red/blue borders
2. Section cards row (3 cards): Employee List, Student List, Card Designs
   - Each links to the section with icon + brief description
3. Needs Attention table: employees/students with expired or expiring IDs
   - Compact table showing name, type, expiration date, status
   - Links to detail page on row click

Dependencies: Phase 1 (sidebar must be in place first so dashboard renders in new shell)

Files:
- NEW: app/routes/admin/index.tsx

title: Phase 3: Split Focus layout for Employee list + detail
description:
Convert the employee list + detail pages into a persistent master-detail (split focus) layout.

Current state:
- /admin/employees renders a full-page table with search/filters
- Clicking a row opens /admin/employees/:id as a separate full page
- The EmployeeQuickViewDrawer exists but is a temporary Sheet overlay, not persistent
- Going back to the list loses scroll position and selection state

Target architecture:
Use React Router nested routes to render the list and detail side by side.

Option A (preferred): Layout route approach
- app/routes/admin/employees.tsx becomes a LAYOUT route that renders the split
- Left panel: scrollable filterable list (derived from current index.tsx)
- Right panel: <Outlet /> renders the detail ($employeeId.tsx) or empty state
- When no employee selected, right panel shows a prompt to select one

Option B: Client-side split within index.tsx
- Keep current route structure but fetch detail data client-side
- More complex, less aligned with React Router conventions

Implementation details (Option A):
1. NEW: app/routes/admin/employees.tsx (layout route with split panels)
   - Left panel (w-80 to w-96, fixed, border-r, overflow-y-auto):
     * Search input at top (sticky)
     * Scrollable employee list with compact cards
     * Active item highlighted with left-border accent
     * Each item shows: name, status badge, photo indicator, expiration warning
   - Right panel (flex-1, overflow-y-auto):
     * <Outlet /> for nested routes
     * Empty state when no employee selected
   - Both panels scroll independently

2. MODIFY: app/routes/admin/employees/index.tsx
   - Becomes the "no employee selected" empty state
   - Or redirects to first employee

3. KEEP: app/routes/admin/employees/$employeeId.tsx
   - Content stays the same but renders inside the right panel
   - Remove "Back to Employees" link (list is always visible)
   - May need to adjust widths (was full-page, now constrained)

4. Mobile behavior:
   - Below md breakpoint: list is full-width, detail is full-width
   - Use URL-based navigation (standard mobile pattern)
   - No split on mobile

Key considerations:
- Preserve all existing functionality (search, filters, bulk actions, sync)
- List should update when detail actions change data (revalidation)
- Selected employee should be visually highlighted in the list
- URL should reflect selected employee (/admin/employees/abc123)
- Search params (search, status, photo filters) preserved in list panel

title: Phase 4: Split Focus layout for Student list + detail
description:
Mirror the employee split focus layout for students.

Same pattern as Phase 3 but for the student routes:

1. NEW: app/routes/admin/students.tsx (layout route with split panels)
   - Left panel: scrollable filterable student list
   - Right panel: <Outlet /> for student detail or empty state
   - Items show: name, grade, status badge, photo indicator

2. MODIFY: app/routes/admin/students/index.tsx
   - Becomes empty state / no-selection view

3. KEEP: app/routes/admin/students/$studentId.tsx
   - Renders in right panel, remove "Back to Students" link
   - Adjust widths for constrained panel

4. Mobile: same as employees — full-width list, full-width detail

This depends on Phase 3 because:
- We can copy the pattern directly once employees are working
- Shared components (list item, empty state) can be extracted during Phase 3
- Reduces duplication risk by doing employees first as the template

Potential shared extractions after both phases:
- SplitFocusLayout component (left/right panels with responsive behavior)
- CompactListItem component (avatar, name, badges row)
- EmptyDetailState component (icon + prompt text)

title: Phase 5: Polish, cleanup, and responsive QA
description:
Final polish pass after all layout changes are in place.

Tasks:
1. Remove the layout-explorations route and component (served its purpose)
2. Remove or deprecate old SidebarNav if fully replaced
3. Responsive testing:
   - Mobile (<640px): verify Sheet nav, full-width pages, sticky action bars
   - Tablet (640-1024px): verify sidebar collapse behavior
   - Desktop (>1024px): verify split focus panels, dashboard grid
4. Dark mode pass: ensure all new components respect dark theme tokens
5. Keyboard accessibility: sidebar expand/collapse, list navigation, focus management
6. Performance check: no layout shift on sidebar expand, smooth transitions
7. Update AdminShell nav items if any routes changed
8. Clean up any TODO comments from implementation phases
